title: Concentration and Nucleic Acid Extraction of Viruses from Airplane Waste
author: Ari N Machtinger

steps.title: Prepare a 10 % volume Tween 20 stock solution
steps.instruction: Prepare a 10 % volume Tween 20 stock solution. For a 40 mL stock, combine 4 mL of Tween 20 with 36 mL of 1X PBS. Filter sterilize with a 0.22 um vacuum filtration unit. This recipe produces enough 10% Tween 20/PBS solution for approximately 20 dissociation treatments.

steps.title: Refer to the 'Safety Warnings' section...
steps.instruction: Refer to the 'Safety Warnings' section for biosafety precautions necessary for handling raw wastewater samples. Transfer the waste sample (within secondary container) from the refrigerator to the fume hood. Remove the sample bottle from the secondary container and unseal the bottle by removing the affixed Parafilm.

steps.title: Prepare aliquots of aggregated airplane waste
steps.instruction: Prepare aliquots of aggregated airplane waste. Invert the bottle of waste several times to re-suspend contents, then carefully open it. Using a fresh 50 mL serological pipette, aspirate and dispense 20 mL of waste into two 50 mL centrifuge tubes each. Repeat for desired number of sample replicates. For a negative control, add 20 mL of 1X PBS to two centrifuge tubes each using a fresh 50 mL serological pipette, for a total volume of 40 mL . The 40 mL sample is separated into two 50 mL tubes, each containing 20 mL of airplane waste and 20 mL of dilution and dissociation reagent (step 4). Additionally, EHS recommended limiting sample volume to 40 mL in each tube to reduce the risk of leaks.

steps.title: Cap and parafilm all bottles and centrifuge tubes
steps.instruction: Cap and parafilm all bottles and centrifuge tubes. Return the waste bottle to secondary containment, wipe exposed surfaces with pre-empt wipes, and return the remaining waste to refrigeration.

steps.title: Place all centrifuge tubes on a...
steps.instruction: Place all centrifuge tubes on a vortexer using a 50 mL tube adapter. Shake at maximum speed for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Transfer all centrifuge tubes from the...
steps.instruction: Transfer all centrifuge tubes from the vortexer to a sonication bath, and sonicate for 00:01:00 at 40 kHz. Use a paper towel to dry the tubes when finished.

steps.title: Prepare vacuum filtration units
steps.instruction: Prepare vacuum filtration units. One pre-filter/filter unit will be used for each waste sample centrifuge tube, so for one replicate of waste (two waste sample centrifuge tubes), prepare two pre-filter/filter units. One pre-filter/filter unit will be used for each negative control sample, so for one negative control replicate (two centrifuge tubes), prepare one pre-filter/filter units.

steps.title: Perform the "Start-up" protocol for the InnovaPrep Concentrating Pipette Select
steps.instruction: Perform the "Start-up" protocol for the InnovaPrep Concentrating Pipette Select. Turn on the Concentrating Pipette, and navigate to "Maintenance" and then "Start-up". Follow the prompts. Check that the maintenance tip is in place. Place the waste line in the proper position. Remove the storage fluid line and insert the foam elution canister. Ensure that the screen reads "WWULTRA".

steps.title: Run the concentration protocol for the filtered waste sample
steps.instruction: Run the concentration protocol for the filtered waste sample.

steps.title: Remove the maintenance tip and place...
steps.instruction: Remove the maintenance tip and place a fresh Ultra CPT into the tip port. Lower the tip into the sample. Ensure that the Tip is as close to the bottom of the sample bottle as possible. If necessary, the bottle can be balanced on its edge while a weighted object holds down the top of the Concentrating pipette. A bottle of PBS can be used as the weighted object.

steps.title: Press "Start Run", allow the Concentrating...
steps.instruction: Press "Start Run", allow the Concentrating Pipette to aspirate the waste, and wait until it beeps to signal the end of the run. A timer will run on the display during the concentration. The Concentrating Pipette will stop aspirating and begin to beep when the Tip detects air instead of liquid sample. Concentration should take roughly 5 min, but can vary based on the consistency of the sample.

steps.title: While holding a 5 mL centrifuge...
steps.instruction: While holding a 5 mL centrifuge tube under the Tip, press "Elute" and catch the foam that is dispensed.

steps.title: After the foam degasses, add 400...
steps.instruction: After the foam degasses, add 400 µL of the Zymo DNA/RNA shield reagent. The eluate should be roughly 400 uL. The Zymo DNA/RNA shield reagent is added at a 1:1 ratio of sample:reagent.

steps.title: Run the concentration protocol for the negative control
steps.instruction: Run the concentration protocol for the negative control. Perform as is done for the waste sample. Make sure to use a fresh Tip for each sample.

steps.title: Incubate samples with added Zymo DNA/RNA...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples with added Zymo DNA/RNA shield reagent at room temperature for 00:30:00 . If samples are sitting for longer then store at 4 °C

steps.title: Perform the "Shut Down" protocol for...
steps.instruction: Perform the "Shut Down" protocol for the InnovaPrep Concentrating Pipette Select. Navigate to "Maintenance" and then "Shut Down". Place the maintenance tip into the tip port. Remove the elution canister. Check to ensure that there is adequate storage fluid and insert the storage fluid line. Turn off the device and remove the waste line.

steps.title: Gather the materials and reagents for...
steps.instruction: Gather the materials and reagents for the Zymo quick-DNA/RNA Viral Kit in the Biosafety Cabinet. Equilibrate samples to room temperature. Refer to the "Guidelines" section for instructions on processing and handling RNA samples.

steps.title: Add 1600 µL of Viral DNA/RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 1600 µL of Viral DNA/RNA buffer to the sample (a 2:1 ratio) and vortex briefly to mix. The volume is scaled according to the manufacturers recommendation.

steps.title: Transfer up to 700 µL of...
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 700 µL of lysate into a Zymo-SpinTM IIC-XLR Column in a collection Tube and centrifuge for 12.000 x g, , 2 minutes . Discard the flow-through in the miniprep waste container. Repeat until full lysate volume is processed.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Viral Wash...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Viral Wash Buffer to the column, centrifuge for 12.000 x g, , 30 seconds and discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Repeat the previous step
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous step.

steps.title: Add 500 µL ethanol (95-100%) to...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL ethanol (95-100%) to the column and centrifuge for 12000 x g, , 1 minute to ensure complete removal of the wash buffer.

steps.title: Transfer the column to a clean...
steps.instruction: Transfer the column to a clean collection tube, and centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, 1 minute to remove any remaining ETOH. This step is not included in the Zymo manual, but reduces the chance of ETOH carryover and subsequent inhibition of downstream enzymatic steps.

steps.title: Carefully, transfer the column into a 1
steps.instruction: Carefully, transfer the column into a 1.5 mL nuclease-free tube.

steps.title: Add 50 µL DNase/RNase-Free Water directly...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL DNase/RNase-Free Water directly to the column matrix and incubate at RT for 00:01:00 . Centrifuge for 12000 x g, , 30 seconds to collect the eluate.

steps.title: Place all extracted nucleic acids in...
steps.instruction: Place all extracted nucleic acids in a freezer set to -80 °C Optional: Perform nucleic acid quantification prior to freezing. Perform RNA or DNA quantification using the Qubit HS RNA assay kit or the Qubit 1X dsDNA Assay kit.

steps.title: Place a 2 um pre-filter in a 0
steps.instruction: Place a 2 um pre-filter in a 0.45 um vacuum filtration cup. Repeat for all pre-filter/filter units needed. Cytiva Whatman Glass Microfiber Prefilters are oriented in the box as they should be placed in the filtration cup. Ensure that the pre-filter is flush with the surface of the filtration cup, and also centered such that sample liquid cannot seep past the pre-filter's edges. Tweezers are useful for proper placement.

steps.title: Perform filtration for the waste sample
steps.instruction: Perform filtration for the waste sample. Waste sample tubes are filtered separately because they easily clog the pre-filter/filter units. Filtrate is combined into one bottle prior to concentration in order to increase the concentration of viral particles in the concentrated eluate. Centrifuge tubes contain a total of 80 mL of sample (40 mL x 2 tubes). Each tube is filtered with a new filter and pre-filter, and the total volume is collected into one collection bottle.

steps.title: When filtration is complete, turn off...
steps.instruction: When filtration is complete, turn off the vacuum line and replace the used pre-filter/filter unit with a fresh one by attaching it to the same pyrex bottle with the waste sample filtrate. Decant the second waste sample tube into the fresh filtration cup. Begin vacuum filtration by capping the vacuum filtration top and opening the vacuum line. When complete, cap the pyrex bottle and set aside. A filtration cup and pre-filter which are saturated in waste may smell unpleasant. To mitigate this, seal the filtration cup in a bag prior to discarding into a larger biowaste bin.

steps.title: Perform filtration for the negative control sample
steps.instruction: Perform filtration for the negative control sample.

steps.title: Attach one pre-filter/filter unit to a...
steps.instruction: Attach one pre-filter/filter unit to a clean 100 mL pyrex bottle. Decant one waste sample tube into the filtration cup. Begin vacuum filtration by capping the vacuum filtration top and opening the vacuum line. The filtrate volume will vary depending on the amount of solid material in the sample. Filtration may occur slowly such filtrate drips at a low yet consistent rate.

steps.title: Attach one pre-filter/filter unit to a...
steps.instruction: Attach one pre-filter/filter unit to a clean 100 mL pyrex bottle. Decant both centrifuge tubes into the filtration cup. Begin vacuum filtration by capping the vacuum filtration top and opening the vacuum line. When complete, cap the pyrex bottle and set aside.

steps.title: Add 18 mL of 1X PBS to each centrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Add 18 mL of 1X PBS to each centrifuge tube. 2 mL of 10 % volume Tween 20 stock solution to each centrifuge tube for a final concentration of 5 % (v/v) Tween 20.
